description: HF x Gir/Kankrej 50% exotic crossbreed — most prevalent commercial dairy animal in Gujarat/Maharashtra cooperatives. BW and requirements per ICAR 2013 worked example (450kg 10kg milk 4% fat).

Pure Gir (Bos indicus) — indigenous milch breed of Saurashtra Gujarat. Adult cow BW 313-475 kg (CBF records); typical managed farm cow ~390 kg. Milk 6-12 L/day; 4.6% fat per ICAR-NBAGR. Requirements from ICAR 2013 factorial tables.
Jersey x Sahiwal/Gir 50% exotic crossbreed — common in Maharashtra cooperative dairies; smaller frame, higher fat. BW ~350 kg. Milk 8-10 L/day at 5% fat. Requirements from ICAR 2013 factorial tables.

Pure Kankrej (dual-purpose Bos indicus) — native to Kankrej Taluka, Banaskantha district, Gujarat. Cow BW ~400-450 kg; milk 5-8 L/day; 4.2% fat. ICAR-NBAGR records: 1300-3600 kg/lactation. Requirements from ICAR 2013 factorial tables.

Pure Sahiwal — high-yielding Bos indicus; occasionally in Maharashtra cooperative herds. Cow BW ~430-450 kg; milk 8-12 L/day; 4.8% fat (Sahiwal dairy records). Requirements from ICAR 2013 factorial tables.
